introRows.html: This Part compiles the most frequently referenced rules, sequences and tables into a single location for quick reference during play. It is not a substitute for the full rules, but it should answer most questions that arise mid-game without the need to search through earlier Parts.
introRows.plain: This Part compiles the most frequently referenced rules, sequences and tables into a single location for quick reference during play. It is not a substitute for the full rules, but it should answer most questions that arise mid-game without the need to search through earlier Parts.

rows.html: Select a Mission Card and a Deployment Card.
rows.plain: Select a Mission Card and a Deployment Card .

rows.html: Set Mission Markers at the coordinates on the Deployment Card.
rows.plain: Set Mission Markers at the coordinates on the Deployment Card .

rows.html: Set Terrain pieces (balanced across four quarters, at least 3" between major structures).
rows.plain: Set Terrain pieces (balanced across four quarters, at least 3" between major structures).

rows.html: Roll-Off - winner assigns the First Player Marker for Round 1.
rows.plain: Roll-Off - winner assigns the First Player Marker for Round 1.

rows.html: Actions (choose one per Unit):
rows.plain: Actions (choose one per Unit):

rows.html: Move: Move across the Battlefield.
rows.plain: Move : Move across the Battlefield.

rows.html: Deploy: Move from Reserves via Entry Edge (must have Available Supply; cannot end in Enemy Zone of Influence) to the Battlefield.
rows.plain: Deploy : Move from Reserves via Entry Edge ( must have Available Supply ; cannot end in Enemy Zone of Influence ) to the Battlefield.

rows.html: Disengage: If Engaged - move out of combat. Cannot perform Ranged Attack or Charge next Phase unless own Supply > Enemy Combined Supply.
rows.plain: Disengage : If Engaged - move out of combat. Cannot perform Ranged Attack or Charge next Phase unless own Supply > Enemy Combined Supply.

rows.html: Hold: Do nothing.
rows.plain: Hold : Do nothing.

rows.html: First to Pass takes First Player Marker for Phase 2.
rows.plain: First to Pass takes First Player Marker for Phase 2.

rows.html: Moving a Unit:Leading Model moves up to Speed. Set the rest of the unitInCoherency.
rows.plain: Moving a Unit: Leading Model moves up to Speed. Set the rest of the unit In Coherency .

rows.html: Available Supply =Supply Pool−Total Supply ofFriendlyUnits on the Battlefield.
rows.plain: Available Supply = Supply Pool − Total Supply of Friendly Units on the Battlefield.

rows.html: Actions (choose one per Unit):
rows.plain: Actions (choose one per Unit):

rows.html: Ranged Attack: Use ranged weapons to attack Enemies.
rows.plain: Ranged Attack : Use ranged weapons to attack Enemies .

rows.html: Charge: Move to Engage Enemies.
rows.plain: Charge : Move to Engage Enemies .

rows.html: Run: Move across the battlefield.
rows.plain: Run: Move across the battlefield.

rows.html: Hold: Do nothing.
rows.plain: Hold : Do nothing.

rows.html: First to Pass takes First Player Marker for Phase 3.
rows.plain: First to Pass takes First Player Marker for Phase 3.

rows.html:
Ranged Attack:

  1. Declare a target: Select anEnemynotEngagedwith any other Unit. Check the weapon’s Range,LoSand if the Target’s tag matches.

  2. Build Attack Pool: SumRoAof eligible models + modifiers +SurgeDie(if weapon has S Dice).

  3. Roll to Hit: Each die result equal to or higher than the Hit characteristic is a success and moves to theArmour Pool.

  4. Resolve Surge: If the target’s Combat Tag matches the weapon’sSurge Type, a number of dice equal to theSurge Dieare not included in theArmour Roll.

  5. Armour Roll:Each die result equal to or higher than theArmourcharacteristic is saved and discarded.

  6. Evade Roll:If eligible, each die result equal to or higher than theEvadecharacteristic is saved and discarded.

  7. Damage & Casualties:Multiply all remaining dice by the weapon’sDamage characteristic. Remove models accordingly.
rows.plain: Ranged Attack : Declare a target: Select an Enemy not Engaged with any other Unit. Check the weapon’s Range, LoS and if the Target’s tag matches. Build Attack Pool: Sum RoA of eligible models + modifiers + Surge Die (if weapon has S Dice ). Roll to Hit: Each die result equal to or higher than the Hit characteristic is a success and moves to the Armour Pool . Resolve Surge: If the target’s Combat Tag matches the weapon’s Surge Type , a number of dice equal to the Surge Die are not included in the Armour Roll . Armour Roll: Each die result equal to or higher than the Armour characteristic is saved and discarded. Evade Roll: If eligible, each die result equal to or higher than the Evade characteristic is saved and discarded. Damage & Casualties: Multiply all remaining dice by the weapon’s Damage characteristic. Remove models accordingly.

rows.html:
Charge:

  1. Declare Targets: Select anEnemy Ground Units (noLoSneeded).

  2. Determine Charge Distance:Roll a D6; theLeading Model moves up to Speed + the result.

  3. Success: If the leading model is able to end a movementWithin theEngagement Range (1") of all targets, move it. Set the rest of the unitsquadIn Coherency, following the priority:

    1. Base-to-Base,

    2. Engagement Range,

    3. Coherency.

  4. Fail:Unit stays, activation ends.
rows.plain: Charge : Declare Targets: Select an Enemy Ground Units (no LoS needed). Determine Charge Distance: Roll a D6; the Leading Model moves up to Speed + the result. Success: If the leading model is able to end a movement Within the Engagement Range (1") of all targets, move it. Set the rest of the unitsquad In Coherency , following the priority : Base-to-Base, Engagement Range , Coherency. Fail: Unit stays, activation ends.

rows.html: All Engaged Unitsmust activate. Players alternate.
rows.plain: All Engaged Units must activate. Players alternate.

rows.html: Actions:
rows.plain: Actions:

rows.html: Close Combat Attack: Use a Close Combat weapon to attack Enemies.
rows.plain: Close Combat Attack: Use a Close Combat weapon to attack Enemies .

rows.html: Pass only when no Engaged Units remain.
rows.plain: Pass only when no Engaged Units remain.

rows.html:
Close Combat Attack:

  1. Close Ranks (Optional): MoveLeading Model up to 3" -must end closer to the enemy. Reposition the rest of the Unit for maximumBase-to-Base.

  2. Declare Ranks: Fighting Rank (Within 1") + Supporting Rank (Base-to-Base with Fighting Rank model). Models in those ranks are eligible for attacking in step 3.

  3. Roll Attacks: Follow theRanged Attack sequence, but as a target, choose anEnemyEngagedwith this Unit.

  4. Remove Casualties: UseEngaged Unit priority (Priority 1 → 2 → 3).
rows.plain: Close Combat Attack: Close Ranks (Optional): Move Leading Model up to 3" - must end closer to the enemy . Reposition the rest of the Unit for maximum Base-to-Base . Declare Ranks: Fighting Rank ( Within 1" ) + Supporting Rank ( Base-to-Base with Fighting Rank model). Models in those ranks are eligible for attacking in step 3. Roll Attacks: Follow the Ranged Attack sequence, but as a target, choose an Enemy Engaged with this Unit. Remove Casualties: Use Engaged Unit priority (Priority 1 → 2 → 3).

rows.html: Control: Sum Supply Within 3" of each Marker. Higher total controls. Ties = Contested.
rows.plain: Control: Sum Supply Within 3" of each Marker. Higher total controls. Ties = Contested.

rows.html: Score VP: Per Mission Card conditions.
rows.plain: Score VP: Per Mission Card conditions.

rows.html: End of Game: Check winning conditions.
rows.plain: End of Game: Check winning conditions.

rows.html: End of the Round Effects: Resolve triggered abilities.
rows.plain: End of the Round Effects: Resolve triggered abilities.

rows.html: Cleanup: Remove Tokens/Markers (keep STAY IN PLAY, Damage Markers, Mission Markers, Faction Indicator s). Refresh all Exhausted cards.
rows.plain: Cleanup: Remove Tokens / Markers (keep STAY IN PLAY , Damage Markers , Mission Markers , Faction Indicator s). Refresh all Exhausted cards.

rows.html: Initiative: Fewer VP = First Player Marker. Tie = Roll-Off.
rows.plain: Initiative: Fewer VP = First Player Marker . Tie = Roll-Off.

rows.plain: Unengaged target:

rows.html: Cannot remove more models than are Visible to the attacker.
rows.plain: Cannot remove more models than are Visible to the attacker.

rows.html: Excess Damage beyond Visible kills is discarded.
rows.plain: Excess Damage beyond Visible kills is discarded.

rows.html: Engaged target (strict order):
rows.plain: Engaged target (strict order):

rows.html: Models not Within the Engagement Range of any Enemy Unit.
rows.plain: Models not Within the Engagement Range of any Enemy Unit.

rows.html: ModelsWithin the Engagement Range but not in Base-to-Base contact.
rows.plain: Models Within the Engagement Range but not in Base-to-Base contact.

rows.html: Models in Base-to-Base contact with an Enemy model.
rows.plain: Models in Base-to-Base contact with an Enemy model.

rows.html: Cannot remove a model if doing so breaks Engagement with an enemy, provided another valid casualty exists.
rows.plain: Cannot remove a model if doing so breaks Engagement with an enemy , provided another valid casualty exists.

rows.html: If a rules dispute cannot be resolved swiftly:
rows.plain: If a rules dispute cannot be resolved swiftly:

rows.html: Both players Roll-Off (2D6). Higher total wins.
rows.plain: Both players Roll-Off (2D6). Higher total wins.

rows.html: The winner decides how the rule applies in this specific instance.
rows.plain: The winner decides how the rule applies in this specific instance.

rows.html: Continue playing. Verify the ruling after the match.
rows.plain: Continue playing. Verify the ruling after the match.

rows.html: This rule exists to keep the game moving, not to provide a tactical loophole.
rows.plain: This rule exists to keep the game moving, not to provide a tactical loophole.

rows.html: This section lists the Mineral Cost and available Composition Options for each Unit in the game. When mustering a Unit during Army Building (Part 9. 1. 6), select one Composition Option from the Unit’s entry below. The chosen option determines the number of models in the Unit, the Mineral Cost paid, and the Unit’s initial Supply Value as shown on its Unit Card’s Supply Profile.
rows.plain: This section lists the Mineral Cost and available Composition Options for each Unit in the game. When mustering a Unit during Army Building (Part 9. 1. 6 ), select one Composition Option from the Unit’s entry below. The chosen option determines the number of models in the Unit, the Mineral Cost paid, and the Unit’s initial Supply Value as shown on its Unit Card’s Supply Profile .

rows.html: Upgrade costs are listed separately beneath each Unit’s Composition Options. A purchased upgrade applies to every model in the Unit unless it has the SPECIALIST keyword (Part 9. 1. 7).
rows.plain: Upgrade costs are listed separately beneath each Unit’s Composition Options. A purchased upgrade applies to every model in the Unit unless it has the SPECIALIST keyword (Part 9. 1. 7 ).
